updates.title: Introducing Claude Tag: Anthropic Launches Persistent AI Teammate for Slack
updates.summary: Anthropic launched Claude Tag on June 23, 2026 — a new product that embeds Claude directly into Slack as a persistent, shared AI team member. Teams invite Claude to specific channels and delegate tasks by typing @Claude; Claude then independently breaks the task into stages, works through them, and delivers the result back in Slack. Unlike individual chatbot interactions, Claude Tag has a shared identity visible to all channel members, allowing teammates to hand off in-progress work and build shared context over time. The product runs on Claude Opus 4.8 and is available in research preview for Claude Enterprise and Claude Team customers, with a 30-day migration window planned for existing Claude-in-Slack users.
updates.accuracy_notes: Confirmed across multiple independent reputable sources all dated June 23, 2026: Fortune, Bloomberg, Engadget, The Next Web, Neowin, SiliconANGLE, Benzinga. Official Anthropic blog post exists at anthropic.com/news/introducing-claude-tag. All key facts (Opus 4.8, Enterprise/Team research preview, @Claude invocation, shared identity model, 30-day migration window) are consistent across sources. No red flags or contradictions detected.

updates.title: Claude Code v2.1.187 — Credential Security, Org Model Controls & 15+ Bug Fixes
updates.summary: Anthropic shipped Claude Code version 2.1.187 on June 23, 2026, with meaningful security and reliability improvements. The headline addition is a new sandbox.credentials setting that prevents sandboxed commands from reading credential files and secret environment variables — directly addressing a key enterprise security concern. The release also adds org-level model restriction controls visible in the model picker with clear 'restricted by your organization's settings' messaging, and mouse click support for menus in fullscreen mode. Over 15 bugs were fixed including --resume failures on pipeline runs, structured output loops in workflow agents, remote MCP tool calls hanging for 5 minutes, and Korean/CJK text encoding corruption in certain terminals.
updates.accuracy_notes: Sourced directly from the official Claude Code changelog at code.claude.com/docs/en/changelog, which loaded successfully. Version 2.1.187 and June 23, 2026 date confirmed in the changelog. All listed features and fixes match what was returned from the live page. Technical claims are straightforward release notes — no marketing embellishment or unverified assertions.
